The '''JMM assassin droid''' was a model of [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin]]<ref name="Barely Tolerable" />/[[espionage droid]]<ref name="Katarn's Tale">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale4|series=Kyle Katarn's Tale|text=Mission 4}}</ref> produced during the era of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="JMM series droid">{{CSWECite|II|168|JMM series droid}}</ref>

A JMM's head weighed ten [[kilogram/Legends|kilos]].<ref name="RA">''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''</ref>

The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]] [[8t88/Legends|8t88]] had his head replaced by that of a JMM droid as a prank.<ref name="Katarn's Tale" /> The head was cut off and was swallowed by his pet, [[Grendel]].<ref name="RA" /> Eventually recovered by [[Kyle Katarn]], who retrieved the coordinates of the [[Valley of the Jedi]] held in its memory banks.<ref name="JK" /> In the process, he used the droid's head to strike [[Picaroon C. Boodle|Pic]] who was jumping on him; the hit smashed him into a bulkhead like a rag doll, killing him.<ref name="RA" />

[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Bertroff Hissa]] was given the arms of a JMM droid after he lost his own during an accident.<ref name="Queen">''[[Queen of the Empire]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:8t88.png|thumb|right|250px|Droid information broker 8t88 had the head of a JMM droid.]]
The first mention of this droid in ''[[Kyle Katarn's Tale]]'' identifies it as an espionage droid, while ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' and ''[[Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire]]'' use the term assassin droid.
